_template_note: This is the empty starter for a project's signal vocabulary. Run the Vocabulary Session per sdd-kit/grammar/BOOTSTRAP.md to populate it. The result becomes the project's signals/0.1.json (not this template file; copy this to signals/0.1.json first and edit there). Once locked, signals/0.1.json is the contract — edits go through the supervised-grammar-evolution proposal taxonomy and bump the version.

ontology._comment: Layer 0 — what kinds of things exist in this system. Per grammar/PER_LAYER.md Layer 0.

categories._comment: Add project-specific architectural-boundary categories below (e.g., for a UI project: 'view'; for a trading project: 'ingest', 'encode', 'execute'; for a game: 'narrative', 'world', 'bird'). Categories align with architectural boundaries, not class names.

tags._template_example: Replace this example block with real tags from the Vocabulary Session. Format mirrors signals/0.1.json from existing projects.
tags.name: EXAMPLE_TAG_TO_REPLACE
tags.note: One-sentence description of what this tag means and when it fires. The note is read by future sessions; write it for them.

sessions._comment: Layer 3 — bounded event streams. Per grammar/PER_LAYER.md Layer 3.

temporal_invariants._comment: Layer 4 — timing invariants. Per grammar/PER_LAYER.md Layer 4.

state_transitions._comment: Layer 5 — ordering rules without timing. Per grammar/PER_LAYER.md Layer 5.

operators._comment: Layer 6 — runtime operators. Per grammar/PER_LAYER.md Layer 6.

evidence_constraints._comment: Layer 7 — payload constraints beyond type signature. Per grammar/PER_LAYER.md Layer 7.

report_binding._comment: Layer 8 — how the project's tag set binds into the Signal Report template. See sdd-kit/templates/SIGNAL_REPORT.md.

grammar_growth._comment: Layer 10 — meta-rules for how the grammar evolves. Per grammar/PRINCIPLES.md proposal taxonomy.

view_payload_universal._comment: If the project has UI, every view-category tag inherits these four structural fields by convention. Per grammar/EXAMPLES.md Example 6 (SCENE_RENDERED). Remove this block if the project has no view category.
